title: Neon Rip off Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Neon Rip-off Evangelion             Quote Post by Ryo » Tue Apr 19, 2005 11:58 am Ladies and Gentlemen, get ready for Revelation: The Awakening!! In the year 1994, a giant meteorite impacted with the Wuhan prefecture in China. This disaster instantly sparked a massive conflict that became known as World War Three. Six years later, the war ended as suddenly as it began, leaving the world to recover from the brink of destruction. The official explanation for the Wuhan disaster provided the people with the answer they expected, and the nations returned to their peaceful hibernation of rebuilding after the war. America was left badly damaged from the worldwide confrontation, with most of its coastal cities abandoned for the new metropolises that were built inland. The year is now 2021 A.D., and the peaceful calm is suddenly shattered by the appearance of the â€œXenoframesâ€, a race of advanced, cybernetic entities hell-bent on the destruction of the world. The only thing that stands in their way of conquest is the organization Nova and their Revenant robots. Piloted by a small group of inexperienced teenagers, these giant machines are the only means of destroying the approaching alien threat, which will stop at nothing to fulfill its insidious task. Prepare yourself for a confrontation of epic proportions in the first volume of Revelation: The Awakening!

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Tue Apr 19, 2005 1:50 pm Hey, is this a book? or something? Cuz if it's a book I totally want to read it! Ok found it on B&N http://search.barnesandnoble.com/booksearch/isbnInquiry.asp?userid=CB6tm2IBlQ&isbn=0595241204&itm=2 It looks to me that this dude got his fanfiction published... Further more, if you look here http://www.sffworld.com/forums/archive/index.php/t-4126.html someone mentions this site www.revelation.cyril.com hmmm, that's odd...I guess this is the dudes site...doesn't mention the book though...

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Tue Apr 19, 2005 8:59 pm Seriously, what the crap?? I can't find anything else online about it, but it does look like it was a fan fiction previously and then it got a makeover...

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Wed Apr 20, 2005 10:39 am i wonder why gainex haven't started sueing people for that sort of thing; there's definately enough matirial to get started with

[]:             Quote Post by Hand of God » Wed Apr 20, 2005 7:16 pm :? what the !!! what a rip off............ is it in tv? ps: yey for my new avatar

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Thu Apr 21, 2005 3:28 pm Hand of God wrote: ps: yey for my new avatar

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Thu Apr 21, 2005 4:58 pm It is slightly anoying, but as for the extended downloading time thing, it's only 19.5 kb's big...but then again I'm on DSL....

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Tue May 03, 2005 7:11 pm Cable pwns dial-up BTW, anyone read the book?

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Tue May 03, 2005 9:13 pm It's my understanding this started as a fan-fic, any body got it, I'm kinda interested, I mean it can't be complete crap...It did get published....*then again....*

[]:             Quote Post by Misuzu » Wed May 25, 2005 4:42 pm anatrok wrote: It's my understanding this started as a fan-fic, any body got it, I'm kinda interested, I mean it can't be complete crap...It did get published....*then again....*

[]:             Quote Post by Oroboro » Thu May 26, 2005 9:02 am Oh god...Has this man no shame? Why must he blatently rip-off Evangelion? Even the cover a poorly drawn anime-style things on it! My eyes!

[]:             Quote Post by Aubrey » Tue May 31, 2005 9:47 pm Yeesh, that mecha on the book's over even looks like an Eva! He didn't have try to make this look original. Is Writer's Club Press a vanity press? Because something about that company's name makes me wonder. Granted they got him an ISBN number, but surely some vanity presses will do that.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Bud » Fri Jul 01, 2005 12:26 am Please let Shinji's Chinese equivalent be named Wang.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Fri Jul 01, 2005 4:16 pm Mr. Bud wrote: Please let Shinji's Chinese equivalent be named Wang.

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Fri Jul 01, 2005 5:31 pm sadsadshinji wrote: Mr. Bud wrote: Please let Shinji's Chinese equivalent be named Wang.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Jul 01, 2005 6:37 pm ^Not even a Kit Kat and a prono?

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Fri Jul 01, 2005 6:44 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: ^Not even a Kit Kat and a prono?

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Fri Jul 01, 2005 7:13 pm well yes we can't forgot the all powerfull pr0n.

[]:             Quote Post by Oroboro » Sat Jul 02, 2005 12:18 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: ^Not even a Kit Kat and a prono?

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Mon Aug 01, 2005 12:31 pm director anno, while making the series, acknowledged that he was ripping off other series in some ways.
